Complete SCM Workflow

Source: vignettes/articles/complete-workflow.Rmd
complete-workflow.Rmd

Complete Stepwise Covariate Modeling Workflow

This comprehensive guide walks through a complete SCM analysis from start to finish.

Overview

The stepwise covariate modeling (SCM/SCM+) process consists of: 0. Backward elimination :Remove non-significant covariates (optional) 1. Forward Selection: Add covariates that improve model fit 2. Backward Elimination: Remove non-significant covariates 3. Final Model: Select the best parsimonious model

Prerequisites 

library(CovariateSearcher)
library(dplyr)

Step 1: Prepare Your Data

NONMEM Dataset

Your dataset should include: - ID: Subject identifier - TIME: Time points - DV: Dependent variable (concentrations) - EVID: Event ID (0=observation, 1=dose) - Covariates (WT, AGE, SEX, etc.)

# Example dataset structure
head(nonmem_data)
#   ID TIME  DV EVID  WT AGE SEX
#    1  0.0  NA    1  70  45   1
#    1  0.5 5.2    0  70  45   1
#    1  1.0 8.1    0  70  45   1

Covariate Search Configuration

Create a CSV file defining which covariates to test:

# Example: covariate_search.csv
covariate_config <- tribble(
  ~PARAMETER, ~COVARIATE, ~STATUS,        ~FORMULA, ~LEVELS, ~REFERENCE,
  "CL",       "WT",        "con",          "power",      NA,         70,
  "CL",       "AGE",       "con",         "linear",      NA,         50,
  "CL",       "SEX",       "cat",            "cat",   "0;1",          1,
  "V",        "WT",        "con",          "power",      NA,      70
)

write.csv(covariate_config, "data/derived/covariate_search.csv", row.names = FALSE)

Step 2: Initialize Search 

search_state <- initialize_covariate_search(
  base_model_path = "run10",
  data_file_path = "data/derived/analysis.csv",
  covariate_search_path = "data/derived/covariate_search.csv",
  lookup_file = "data/spec/lookup.yaml", # optional
  models_folder = "models", 
  threads = 40)

If you are continuing an existing project with pre-existing models, use:

search_state <- load_existing_search(
  base_model_path = "run10",
  data_file_path = "data/derived/analysis.csv",
  covariate_search_path = "data/derived/covariate_search.csv",
  models_folder = "models",
  threads = 40,
  lookup_file = "data/spec/lookup.yaml" # optional
)

Step 3: Run Forward Selection 

# Standard forward selection
 results <- run_automated_scm_testing(
  search_state = search_state,
  base_model_id = "run10",
  scm_type = "standard",      # or "selective"
  starting_phase = "forward",
  full_scm = FALSE,           # This runs ONLY forward selection
  forward_p_value = 0.05,
  rse_threshold = 50,
  require_cov_step = TRUE,
  auto_submit = TRUE,
  auto_retry = TRUE
)

# Check results
print(results$final_model)
print(results$final_covariates)

Understanding Selection Criteria

Models are selected if they meet BOTH criteria: - ΔOFV > 3.84: Statistically significant improvement (p < 0.05) - RSE < 50%: Parameter estimates are reasonably precise

Step 4: Backward Elimination 

results <- run_automated_scm_testing(
  search_state = search_state,
  base_model_id = "run10",
  starting_phase = "backward",
  full_scm = FALSE,            # This runs ONLY the specified phase
  backward_p_value = 0.01,
  auto_submit = TRUE,
  auto_retry = TRUE
)

print(results$final_model)

Step 5: Full SCM+ Workflow in One Call

Use this when you want a complete SCM+ sequence starting from a developed model:

results <- run_automated_scm_testing(
  search_state = search_state,
  base_model_id = "run10",
  scm_type = "selective",
  starting_phase = "backward",
  full_scm = TRUE,              # Backward -> Forward -> Backward
  forward_p_value = 0.05,
  backward_p_value = 0.01,
  rse_threshold = 50,
  require_cov_step = TRUE,
  auto_submit = TRUE,
  auto_retry = TRUE,
  save_checkpoints = TRUE,
  checkpoint_prefix = "scm_auto"
)

Step 6: Review Results 

search_state <-readRDS("./models/scm_rds/scm_auto_final_complete.rds")
results_table <- create_scm_results_table(search_state)
print_scm_results_table(search_state)

Step 7: Resume from Checkpoints 

search_state <- load_search_state("scm_auto_forward_complete.rds")

results <- run_automated_scm_testing(
  search_state = search_state,
  base_model_id = "run10",
  starting_phase = "backward",
  full_scm = FALSE,
  backward_p_value = 0.01
)

Next Steps
